Reproduction steps: 1. An org2 participant uploads their ID: 2. An admin from another organisation, in this case org1, is able to open the ID from org2 by opening request 35, e.g `http://localhost:3001/admin/id_documents/pending_authorizations/35/confirmations/new` 3. The admin then approves this request by looking up the ID in the picture (not shown in this image, but a real ID would expose this) 4. Now the request has been approved, which can be seen from the org2 participant authorizations page: ### Impact A tenant admin can access, reject or approve another tenant's `id_documents` requests. ### Patches See https://github.com/decidim/decidim/pull/16666 ### Workarounds Disable the "Identity documents" verification ### Reference OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control ### Credits This issue was discovered in a security audit organized by the [Decidim Association](https://decidim.org) and made by [Radically Open Security](https://www.radicallyopensecurity.com/) against Decidim financed by [NGI](https://ngi.eu/).", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-86fh-w43w-338c", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-13T16:54:32Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-86fh-w43w-338c", "identifiers": [{"type":
